LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MBV LAW, LLP, DON BUDER, PAUL NEIBERGS SECOND PETITION TO COMEL ARBITRATION. DENIED. DEFENDANTS BRING A RENEWED MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH CCP SECTION 1008(B). DEFENDANTS DO NOT DEMONSTRATE DILIGENCE OR WHY THESE ISSUES WERE NOT PREVIOUSLY LITIGATED AND FAILS TO PROVIDE THE REQUIRED DECLARATION. THE MOTION ALSO FAILS ON ITS MERITS. PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT SUING ON THE MERGER AGREEMENT. THEY ARE SUING BECAUSE DEFENDANTS ALLEGEDLY BREACHED THEIR INDEPENDENT ATTORNEY/CLIENT DUTY. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. (PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797 (302/CWW)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS DAVID CONDENSA, ROBERT CONDENSA, DAVID AUERWECK, TERRI MARINE AND KEVIN HAWKIN'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTNG DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER [CCP 1008] IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. VARIOUS ORDERS ON OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. ---------------------------- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. WHILE THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS WAS TRYING TO OBTAIN WAIVERS FROM DATALINK JUST PRIOR TO THE HEARING ON THE MOTION FOR THE PROTECTIVE ORDER (A FACT THE COURT FELT SHOULD HAVE BEEN DISCLOSED AT THE HEARING), THE WAIVER WAS NOT SIGNED UNTIL AFTER THE HEARING AND THE COURT'S RULING ON THE ORIGINAL MOTION. AS THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT DATALINK WAS DEFINITELY WILLING TO SIGN THE WAIVER BY THE TIME OF THE HEARING AND RULING, THIS FACT DISTINGUISHES THIS CASE FROM THE FACTS IN THE NY TIMES V. SUP. COURT CASE (2005) 135CAL.APP.4TH 206. FURTHER IT DOES APPEAR THAT THERE WAS SUFFICIENT DUE DILIGENCE EXERCISED BY PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL TO OBTAIN THE WAIVER(S) PRIOR TO THE HEARING ON THE ORIGINAL MOTION. GIVEN THAT THERE ARE NOW WAIVERS OF THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE TO THE ATTORNEYS' FILES, THE COURT VACATES THE PRIOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND AUTHORIZES THE RELEASE OF THE FILES TO PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL. HOWEVER, THE COURT WILL STAY THE RELEASE OF THE FILES PENDING APPELLATE REVIEW OF THE COURT'S DECISION & ORDER. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (NOTE: MOTION HEARD AT 9:30AM WITH OTHER LAW & MOTION MATTER IN SAME CASE.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

Off Calendar May-13-2011 Continued To Jun-28-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS MBV LAW LLP, DONALD BUDER, AND PAUL J. NEIBERGS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Karen M. Goodman, Esq. of Goodman & Associates 916/643-0600 for pltfs & opposing parties; Natalie P. Vance, Esq. & Jason W. Schaff, Esq. of Klinedinst PC 916/444-7573 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: The Court vacated its tentative ruling and continued the matter to 06/28/11 at 9:30am. Plaintiffs order on objections to evidence signed in open court. (Note: Motion heard with discovery matter in same case.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489

Ntc Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Grantng Defts' Motion For Protectiove Order
Off Calendar May-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFFS DAVID CONDENSA, ROBERT CONDENSA, DAVID AUERWECK, TERRI MARINE AND KEVIN HAWKIN'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTNG DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER [Code of Civil Procedure Section 1008].

APPEARANCES: Karen M. Goodman, Esq. of Goodman & Associates 916/643-0600 for pltfs & moving parties; Natalie P. Vance, Esq. & Jason W. Schaff, Esq. of Klinedinst PC 916/444-7573 for defts & opposing parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission. Various orders on objections to evidence signed in open court.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Court grants plaintiffs motion for reconsideration. While the evidence shows that counsel for plaintiffs was trying to obtain waivers from Datalink just prior to the hearing on the motion for the protective order (a fact the Court felt should have been disclosed at the hearing), the waiver was not signed until after the hearing and the Court's ruling on the original motion. As there is no evidence that Datalink was definitely willing to sign the waiver by the time of the hearing and ruling, this fact distinguishes this case from the facts in the NY Times v. Sup. Court case (2005) 135Cal.App.4th 206. Further it does appear that there was sufficient due diligence exercised by plaintiffs counsel to obtain the waiver(s) prior to the hearing on the original motion. Given that there are now waivers of the attorney-client privilege to the attorneys' files, the Court vacates the prior protective order and authorizes the release of the files to plaintiffs' counsel. However, the Court will stay the release of the files pending appellate review of the Court's decision & order. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. (Note: Motion heard at 9:30am with other law & motion matter in same case.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489

Otice Of Petition And Second Petition To Comel Arbitration; Notice Of Lodgement Of Authorities; Second Petition
Off Calendar Nov-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Schaff, Esq. of Klinedinst, 916-444-7573 for defendants and moving parties
MBV LAW, LLP, DON BUDER, PAUL NEIBERGS
Karen Goodman, Esq. of Goodman & Assoc.'s, 916-643-0600 for plaintiff and opposing party David Condesa

RULING:
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
DEFENDANT MBV LAW, LLP, DON BUDER, PAUL NEIBERGS Second Petition To Comel Arbitration.
DENIED. DEFENDANTS BRING A RENEWED MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH CCP SECTION 1008(b). DEFENDANTS DO NOT DEMONSTRATE DILIGENCE OR WHY THESE ISSUES WERE NOT PREVIOUSLY LITIGATED AND FAILS TO PROVIDE THE REQUIRED DECLARATION.
THE MOTION ALSO FAILS ON ITS MERITS. PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT SUING ON THE MERGER AGREEMENT. THEY ARE SUING BECAUSE DEFENDANTS ALLEGEDLY BREACHED THEIR INDEPENDENT ATTORNEY/CLIENT DUTY.
REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. (Plaintiff's counsel to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797 (302/CWW)

DEFENDANTS MBV LAW, LLP, DON BUDER, AND PAUL J. NEIBERGS PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

APPEARANCES: Virginia G. Rogers, Esq. of Goodman & Associates 916/643-0600 for pltfs & opposing parties; Jason W. Schaff, Esq. of Klinedinst PC 916/444-7573 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. DEFENDANTS FAIL TO PROVE THE EXISTENCE OF A VALID ARBITRATION AGREEMENT.
